note: Generated by scripts/build-manifest.mjs — do not hand-edit. Run the script to refresh.

account.name: LoginCard
account.summary: Login — the sign-in card, shown twice: the default state and the error state side by side.

account.name: PaymentBand
account.summary: Payment band — a checkout pairing: card form on the left, plan receipt on the right.

account.name: ProfileCard
account.summary: Profile — an account rendered as a record, not a billboard: a serif name and mono meta over a hairline stat strip, with a recent-activity log underneath that leads with timestamps and follows with verbs.

account.name: SettingsPanel
account.summary: Settings — every preference stacked in one card: label and consequence on the left, control (toggle, segmented switch, or select) on the right, a hairline between each decision.

account.name: SignupCard
account.summary: Sign-up — the account-creation card, same grammar as sign-in with one addition: a live password-strength meter of four hairline segments scored from length and character classes, climbing danger → warn → up as you type.

chatbot.name: ChatComposerReference
chatbot.summary: Chat composer — the terminal prompt input: an auto-growing textarea with a live character counter against a limit and a send affordance; echoes the last sent line back into the scrollback.

chatbot.name: ChatInlineChartReference
chatbot.summary: A chart the assistant can drop straight into a turn — same engine and token discipline as the Finance surfaces, just compact and bubble-framed.

chatbot.name: ChatInlineGraphReference
chatbot.summary: Inline route graph — a small SVG supervisor→agent flow graph rendered inline in an assistant turn, drawing its edges when scrolled into view.

chatbot.name: ChatMarkdownReference
chatbot.summary: Chat markdown — an assistant turn rendering full markdown (headings, emphasis, lists, block quotes, fenced code with a copy affordance, tables) on the token palette.

chatbot.name: ChatThinkingReference
chatbot.summary: Thinking chain — the model's reasoning steps streamed into the transcript as it works, each line revealing when scrolled into view.

chatbot.name: ChatToolCallReference
chatbot.summary: Collapsible tool-call blocks — the terminal-CLI tool chain (the Claude Code / opencode pattern): each call is a mono line (chevron · tool(args) · status · timing) that folds its output away.

chatbot.name: ChatWidgetsReference
chatbot.summary: Chat widgets — custom action widgets embedded in the terminal transcript: a metrics grid (money colors on P&L) and interactive action cards.

chrome.name: AnnouncementBarReference
chrome.summary: Graphite's announcement bar: 48px above the nav, the ENTIRE bar is the link, dismissible, an a11y region.

chrome.name: CommandPaletteReference
chrome.summary: Command palette — the dev-tool ⌘K signature: a fuzzy command bar over a blurred page, opened by keystroke or the trigger button.

chrome.name: FooterReference
chrome.summary: Footer & chrome — utility link columns, a status/theme/copyright row, and the one sanctioned personality moment: an ambient bloom under a giant outlined colophon.

chrome.name: ModuleCardReference
chrome.summary: Module card — the landing-page module tile from @digithings/web, the sibling of Nav/Footer/Colophon in the shared chrome file.

chrome.name: NavShellReference
chrome.summary: Nav shell — the production top-bar primitive from @digithings/web, framed as a static specimen in its two dress states: at rest (transparent over the hero) and settled (hairline + blurred band once the page has scrolled).

chrome.name: ScrollNavReference
chrome.summary: Scroll-aware nav — a sticky bar that gains a hairline and blurred backdrop only after the hero clears, and yields to reading direction: it hides on scroll-down and returns on scroll-up.

chrome.name: TabsReference
chrome.summary: Reusable tab strip with a sliding active indicator.

chrome.name: ToastStackReference
chrome.summary: Toast stack — transient notifications that slide in at a corner, stack, and retire themselves on a watchable timer, or dismiss by hand.

controls.name: AccordionReference
controls.summary: Accordion — a single-open disclosure over the FAQ.

controls.name: ControlsLayerReference
controls.summary: Controls layer — the shipped @digithings/web control atoms in their reference dress, the primitives the marketing sites and digichat both consume.

controls.name: DropdownReference
controls.summary: Dropdown — a custom select whose pane is its own surface: an in-pane filter, grouped options, rich rows (livery dot, description, profit-factor badge), and a footer action.

controls.name: EmptyStatesReference
controls.summary: Empty / error states — the outcomes the skeleton loader resolves into when there's nothing to show: no results, a first-run blank, and a load error.

controls.name: FormFieldsReference
controls.summary: Form fields — labelled inputs in mono micro-caps across every state: focus lights the accent ring, error swaps the border to --down with a message beneath, disabled dims and locks.

controls.name: NavButtonsReference
controls.summary: Navigation buttons — the wayfinding controls: a segmented range switch (the selected cell wears an accent-weak wash), a prev/next pager with a disabled edge state and a current-page marker, and borderless icon buttons.

controls.name: SearchBarReference
controls.summary: Search bar — a search field with a leading glyph, a clear affordance that appears once there's input, and results that resolve live as you type against a small corpus.

controls.name: SkeletonReference
controls.summary: Skeleton loading states — placeholder shapes with a shimmer sweep that stand in until content arrives, then swap 1:1 to the real layout.

controls.name: TagsInputReference
controls.summary: Tags / chips input — the multi-select filter.

controls.name: TooltipReference
controls.summary: Tooltip — a label that surfaces on hover and keyboard focus, fades in with a small rise, and points back at its trigger with a rotated-square arrow.

data.name: CardDeck
data.summary: Card deck — thin consumer of the promoted <DeckStack/> / <DeckCard/> (@digithings/web, #1450): the sticky stacking deck (the cavemen.dev pattern) with the mono progress rail.

data.name: ChangelogRailReference
data.summary: Changelog rail — a horizontal scroll strip of release cards.

data.name: MetricCard
data.summary: Presentational card variants rendered inside CardDeck items — a metric summary, a changelog, a pull-quote, and an alert.

data.name: DotMatrixStat
data.summary: Dot-matrix stat — a single metric rendered as a hardware-style LED grid.

data.name: MarqueeTickerReference
data.summary: Infinite marquee ticker — the scrolling "built on" strip common to the reference sites, in our terminal register: two mono rows drifting in opposite directions, edge-fade masks, seamless loop and pause-on-hover.

data.name: OdometerReference
data.summary: Odometer counter — the mechanical digit-roll (Revolut's sleek counters), the companion to the count-up stat.

data.name: PricingReference
data.summary: Pricing — the open-core two-path shape (self-hosted vs.

data.name: PricingMatrixReference
data.summary: Graphite's pricing system: grouped comparison rows, ✓/Limited/Advanced cells, an annual toggle (20% off), per-tier CTA verbs that escalate with commitment — and enterprise never shows a price.

data.name: RoadmapGanttReference
data.summary: Roadmap Gantt — initiative bars laid across a month axis.

data.name: SortableTableReference
data.summary: Sortable data table — a strategy leaderboard you can reorder by any column.

data.name: StatCounterReference
data.summary: Stat counter — a metric strip whose figures count up from zero the first time the strip scrolls into view, the one place a number earns a little motion.

effects.name: AmbientMesh
effects.summary: Reference port of the ambient background bloom used on digithings.ai subpages (architecture / chat / modules) and, in a teal-palette variant, on digiquant.io subpages (pipeline / strategies / contact).

effects.name: ClipReveal
effects.summary: Graphite's band transition: the incoming section wipes in via a clip-path inset riding the same glide curve as everything else (~0.6s feel).

effects.name: CrossfadeSections
effects.summary: Cross-fade + parallax reveal — the softest of the three between-sections transitions mined from revolut.com.

effects.name: HeroGraphReference
effects.summary: Reveal-field hero graph — the cursor-following node web from the digithings.ai and digiquant.io landing heroes.

effects.name: PipelineReference
effects.summary: Pipeline — a workflow visualization ported from the olympus dashboard's pipeline section (frontend/olympus/components/pipeline).

effects.name: ResearchPipeline
effects.summary: Reference port of the digiquant.io landing `#pipeline` band — the scroll-assembling research → execution walkthrough.

effects.name: RotatingPrompts
effects.summary: x.ai's rotating hero teasers + cursor's codebase Q&A, translated: the prompts are REAL quant questions, and in production each one links into digichat pre-filled — the teaser is a door, not a poster.

effects.name: RoutingMap
effects.summary: Arc-flight routing map — mined from revolut.com's globe (money crossing borders), in our register: orders routing across venues.

effects.name: SectionMorph
effects.summary: Pinned zoom-morph — mined from revolut.com's between-sections transition, where a full-bleed visual scales and rounds down to dock as a card in the next section while that section's copy rises in beside it.

effects.name: StackingPanels
effects.summary: Stacking panels — the second between-sections transition mined from revolut.com.

finance.name: DrawdownPlotReference
finance.summary: Drawdown plot — the underwater curve: percent below the running peak on TradingView Lightweight Charts, hanging under a zero baseline in the `--down` money color (it only ever reads negative).

finance.name: EquityCurveReference
finance.summary: Equity curve — the tearsheet's headline: cumulative equity as an area series on TradingView Lightweight Charts, wearing the module accent (identity, not a P&L read), token-themed with a live retheme on theme and livery flips.

finance.name: MonthlyReturnsReference
finance.summary: Returns matrix — the tearsheet period matrix: months across, years down, each cell tinted by its value on the money tokens — deeper `--up` for stronger gains, `--down` for losses (drawdown and volatility read all-down), empty where there is no data yet — with the year column compounding the total.

finance.name: OrderbookReference
finance.summary: Order book — the exchange-grade depth ladder: asks descending into the spread, a mid/spread divider, then bids below.

finance.name: PerfMetricsReference
finance.summary: Performance metrics — a tearsheet's grade block: labels in mono micro-caps, values in large tabular numerals, laid out on a four-up hairline grid.

finance.name: PerformanceDashboardReference
finance.summary: Performance dashboard — the portfolio at a glance: a value/return headline, a strip of aggregate ratios, and an allocation-by-strategy breakdown.

finance.name: PriceChartReference
finance.summary: Price chart — candlesticks and volume on TradingView Lightweight Charts: candles in the `--up`/`--down` money colors, volume in the hairline token, re-themed live on theme and livery flips.

finance.name: StockTickerReference
finance.summary: Stock ticker tape — the scrolling market strip, in our register.

finance.name: SyncedTearsheetReference
finance.summary: One Lightweight Charts instance, two panes sharing a single time axis: cumulative equity on top, the underwater drawdown below.

foundations.name: ButtonsCtaReference
foundations.summary: Buttons & CTA states — the button vocabulary (primary / ghost / quiet) plus a magnetic CTA that eases toward the pointer and settles back on leave (disabled under reduced motion).

foundations.name: ContentsOverview
foundations.summary: Contents overview — the home-page index of every design family, each a card linking to its page with a one-line blurb.

foundations.name: FeaturePickerReference
foundations.summary: Feature picker — one viewport that tours the modules: pick one from the rail and the panel swaps to its emblem + copy, dressing the section in that module's accent livery.

foundations.name: LiverySwitcher
foundations.summary: Livery switcher — a self-contained swatch strip demoing the per-module accent colours (local state only, not the app-wide store).

foundations.name: ThemeGallery
foundations.summary: Theme gallery — the livery swatches, wired to the same store as the nav's livery selector, so choosing one dresses the whole app and persists across navigation.

layout-patterns.name: BentoGridReference
layout-patterns.summary: Bento grid — an asymmetric module bento: the supervisor anchors a 2×2 hero, the flagship takes a wide cell, the rest fall into units, each wearing its module livery.

layout-patterns.name: DocsLayoutReference
layout-patterns.summary: Docs layout — the shared @digithings/web docs family in one contained specimen: the sticky scroll-spied sidebar (a native <details> disclosure below 860px — the sidebar never just vanishes, canon §17), endpoint chrome with theme-aware HTTP-method chips, and label-tabbed code samples with a hover-revealed copy button.

layout-patterns.name: FeatureCellReference
layout-patterns.summary: Feature cell — a feature block pairing an eyebrow / outcome / mechanism copy column with a scaled product frame.

layout-patterns.name: NumberedStagesReference
layout-patterns.summary: Numbered stages — a Linear-style numbered progression mapped to the digithings pipeline, each stage carrying its module livery, title, and mechanism.

layout-patterns.name: PhoneOlympus
layout-patterns.summary: Phone mockup showcasing the olympus app — a device frame (matte bezel, dynamic-island, side buttons) wrapping a mock olympus mobile dashboard.

layout-patterns.name: MockTearsheet
layout-patterns.summary: ProductFrame — the "real surface, cropped" technique mined from graphite: a product screenshot is authored once on a FIXED 800px artboard, then scaled proportionally to fit its container so it never reflows and stays pixel-faithful at any width.

layout-patterns.name: TestimonialWallReference
layout-patterns.summary: Testimonial wall — a wall of pull-quotes with attribution (illustrative copy; production uses real names per the voice doctrine).

shared.name: SiteNav
shared.summary: Shared top bar for the design-reference app.

symbols.name: Glyph
symbols.summary: Glyph — the interface utility symbol set, as a programmatic name → drawing registry.

symbols.name: OlympusMark
symbols.summary: Product brand marks, ported faithfully for the symbols reference page.

tearsheet.name: TearsheetCardReference
tearsheet.summary: Tearsheet cards — the strategy-library card: the whole card is an anchor with hover-lift and an accent border wake over the gradient surface, a mono name/period head with the pulsing LiveBadge on nightly-refreshed entries, and a 2-column KPI grid whose values wear the money tones.

tearsheet.name: TearsheetChartsReference
tearsheet.summary: Tearsheet charts — the print-grade tearsheet core: a 6-up KPI strip over the tabbed SVG chart deck (candles with trade entry/exit markers + hover cards, equity with a linear/log toggle, underwater drawdown, per-trade P&L with the unrealized open leg, and the 3x3 returns matrix).

tearsheet.name: TearsheetTradeLogReference
tearsheet.summary: Tearsheet trade log — every round trip as a row: direction pill (long wears --up, short --down), dates, entry/mark, and a toned return cell.

terminal.name: CodeReviewReference
terminal.summary: Code review — a unified diff inside a document frame, the proof surface behind an agentic-coding stack.

terminal.name: CodeSampleReference
terminal.summary: Code sample — the developer-docs staple: a copyable command with a package- manager switcher, and a syntax-lit snippet beside it.

terminal.name: StreamTranscriptReference
terminal.summary: Streaming transcript — the digichat grammar (canon §16): `>` user turn, `▸` assistant turn, a folded tool-work chip, and a streaming caret that types the answer token by token.

terminal.name: TerminalBudgetReference
terminal.summary: Terminal + budget sidebar — a diegetic `digithings init` CLI session that types its command and reveals output rows in view, paired with an illustrative context-budget sidebar that lights up each module as its line lands.

terminal.name: TerminalManifestReference
terminal.summary: Terminal manifest — a `digithings ps` process pane: selectable module rows (status dot, two-tone mono name, port, role) beside an output panel that types the selected row's summary at a blinking cursor.

typography.name: CopyGrammarReference
typography.summary: Copy & voice grammar — the headline / subhead formulas the product writes to, each shown as a pattern plus an example, with ours flagged.

typography.name: TypeSpecimen
typography.summary: Type specimen — a canonical surface for evaluating the live type suite (switch it in the nav).

typography.name: WordRevealMuted
typography.summary: Same hold contract as the blur variant: done by 70%, held for the last 30%.

typography.name: WordRevealOutline
typography.summary: The closing word fills with the accent, per the house .colo-word pattern.
